# Adding a Scene

**Article**

Control how the article’s opening section comes into view.

## Overview

The fading sticky header scene causes the header to briefly “stick” to the top of the screen and then fade to a defined color.

**On this page, you’ll learn how to add a scene to your article.**



To implement a scene, you add a `scene` property to a `section` or `chapter` component.

### Apply a Scene

1. In your working `article.json` file, delete the parallax behavior from the first section. The code you are deleting begins with `"behavior": {` and ends with a closing brace and a comma (`},`).
2. Copy the example code [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNews/adding-a-scene#Scene-Copy-This-Code].
3. Paste the code inside the first section, replacing the behavior that you deleted in step 1.

Your code should look like the example code [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNews/adding-a-scene#Scene-Result].

After you make this change in your code, you can preview your working `article.json` file in News Preview to see the fading sticky header scene.

#### Scene: Copy This Code

```json
      "scene": {
        "type": "fading_sticky_header",
        "fadeColor": "#000"
      },
```

#### Scene: Result

Ellipses (`...`) indicate lines of code that have been omitted from this example.

```json
{
  ...
  "components": [
    {
      "role": "section",
      "layout": "fullBleedLayout",
      "scene": {
        "type": "fading_sticky_header",
        "fadeColor": "#000"
      },
      "components": [
        {
          "role": "header",
          "layout": "headerImageLayout",
          "style": {
            "fill": {
              "type": "image",
              "URL": "bundle://header.jpg",
              "fillMode": "cover",
              "verticalAlignment": "top",
              "horizontalAlignment": "center"
            }
          },
          "components": [
            ...
          ]
        }
      ]
    },
    ...
  ],
  ...
}
```

### Previous

[doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNews/adding-animations]

### Next

[doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNews/viewing-the-finished-article-for-advanced-design-tutorial-2]

## Related Documentation

- **Adding a Scene to a Chapter or a Section Header**: Add a scene to your article to create special effects.
- **FadingStickyHeader**: The scene that briefly keeps a header at the top of the screen as the person scrolls through the article.
- **Section**: The component for organizing an article into sections.

## Advanced Design Tutorial 2: Layout and Positioning

- **Creating a Complex, Layered Header**: Layer a title and heading in front of an image, with their colors optimized for legibility.
- **Creating a Floating Caption**: Position a caption in the wide right margin of your article.
- **Creating an Inset Pull Quote**: Wrap article body text around an inset pull quote.
- **Creating an Inset Photo**: Wrap article body text around an inset photo.
- **Adding Color to Text Ranges**: Create text in color by using HTML to refer to TextStyle objects.
- **Adding Animations**: Use animations to affect how parts of your article come into view the first time they appear.
- **Viewing the Finished Article for Advanced Design Tutorial 2**: See the full JSON code from this tutorial.
